reforms.description: The Alianza for Quality Education aims to improve the quality of education in Mexico through various initiatives, including school modernization, teacher professionalization, and curriculum reform.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing teacher quality and school infrastructure, the initiative is expected to improve educational outcomes, which can lead to higher productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative was officially announced and has been in effect since 2008.
reforms.importance_rationale: The Alianza represents a comprehensive approach to reforming the education system, addressing multiple weaknesses and aiming for systemic change in teacher quality and school performance.
reforms.source_quote: In May 2008 President Calderón announced a voluntary agreement between the government and the 1.5 million member teachers’ union to improve the quality of education in Mexico.
